content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660            322-5901            322-6441              322-6444


July 17, 1981


Mr. Hal Evry
74965 Fairway Drive
Palm Desert, CA 92260

Re:  Birthday Gift to Councilmember

Dear Mr. Evry:

    In my letter of July 10, 1981, I failed to mention that
the result is different if the councilmember does not claim
the $800 donation as a charitable contribution for tax
purposes.  Under Government Code Section 82028(b), the term
"gift" does not include:

    (2)  Gifts which are not used and which, within 30
days after receipt, are returned to the donor or
delivered to a charitable organization without
being claimed as a charitable contribution for tax
purposes.

    Consequently, if the youth camp is a charitable organ-
ization and the contribution is not claimed, then there would
be no reporting requirement.

    I hope that this clarifies the situation.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert E. Leidigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL/gs

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Intere
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-3901            322-6441          322-6444

July 10, 1981


Mr. Hal Evry
74965 Fairway Drive
Palm Desert, CA 92260

Re: Birthday Gift to Councilmember

Dear Mr. Evry:

I am responding to your recent request for informal advice
as to reporting requirements where a city councilmember has
received a birthday gift from a group of well wishers.
The gift was $800 cash, which was, in turn, donated by the
councilmember to a youth camp. Under such circumstances,
the $800 should be reported on the next statement of
economic interest as coming from the number of people who
contributed it, pro rated per person. Then a listing of the
names of the contributors.

This letter does not constitute formal advice pursuant
to Government Code Section 83114(b), since our regulations
require that such advice be given only to the councilmember
or his/her agent, and we would need that person's name.
However, it is hoped that this information will satisfy
your needs.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:gs
